series_metadata.series_description: In accordance with section 2937.221 of the Ohio Revised Code, any person arrested under specific traffic violation may post bond by depositing a current valid drivers license to the arresting officer or the court. If the person arrested fails to appear in court at the given time, the license of such person is then forwarded from the court to the Bureau of Motor Vehicles. The BMV notifies the person no valid drivers or commercial drivers license shall be issued to the person after the suspension unless the court having jurisdiction orders the termination of the forfeiture.

retention_rules.disposition: Paper: All hard copy cases closed before July 2, 1990 will be retained at agency storage for one year, then destroyed. The hard copies are destroyed after they are microfilmed and processed film proofread and verified, or after they are scanned into the Optic Image System. ; Microform: All License Forfeiture clearances and suspension notices are stored on Optic Image System beginning in January 2001 for one (1) year from the date the case is closed. ; Electronic: Effective July 2, 1990 all active cases generated before or after this date will be on microfilm-microfiche; any closed cases generated after this date will be on film., Paper: Shred ; Microform: Shred ; Electronic: Delete
